quote_of_day.text: >>19080 >we are simply growing up I've fallen very low at some times in my life, but I never would mock rape victims. If you ever mocked rape victims there is something very intrinsically wrong with your head, the cure of which would be shotgun shell directly to it.
quote_of_day.name: Ambedkar

guest_letters.title: Based website
guest_letters.body: Why aren’t we talking more about this IAC guy?? I just woke uop and checked bahut interesting site hai, there’s quotes and funny articles and board status?? We need more anons like iac to make cool shit. Im a very impressed pajeet. Writing this to see if it will get featured on the newspaper or not, im not sure how he set it up but if the !thing works then hello indus news! Im on da news niggaaaaaa
guest_letters.name: Narthie

articles.title: Weather for Indus today
articles.body:
A low-pressure system of sacrilegious comments moved over the board, bringing scattered thunderstorms of blasphemy and dietary law debates.

Anon clashed over sacred animals and eating beef, with one front of ridicule aimed at those who deify certain species.

A brief but intense heat wave of idol group enthusiasm flared up, with Anon speculating about an Indian girl group and their potential music style.

Meanwhile, a cold snap of caste hatred settled over a part of the board, marked by calls for violence against low castes and a flurry of heated replies.

A drizzle of dark humor and meme debates persisted, including discussions around a sariya meme and rape jokes.

A fog of personal despair rolled in, as one Anon's cries for motivation were met with unusual responses. The atmosphere remained tense, with little sign of clearing.

articles.title: Anon Debates Sacred Animals, Dietary Laws
articles.body:
Ambedkar opened the discussion, calling the concept of a sacred animal 'the most retarded thing a culture could do.' They argued that eating beef is acceptable, as it's 'just a fucking animal,' and suggested that those who consider it sacred should get their 'malnutritioned brain checked.' Anon responded by suggesting that the disagreement stems from different frameworks and moral layers.

They noted that even if one doesn't care about animal welfare, the spiritual layer of belief is a separate issue. Another anon added that criticizing the concept of sacred animals is fair, but abandoning it altogether is not practical.

Anon also brought up the idea that cows are not considered God, but rather an animal meant to be protected. They explained that the concept of Aghniya, or protected cows, has been misunderstood.

A different anon discussed the genetic aspects of the Y chromosome, arguing that it's not weaker than the X chromosome, but rather functions differently. The conversation turned heated when Narthie criticized two other users for 'shitting up' the thread.

Narthie used strong language, calling them 'retards' and 'nigger faggot.' Anon responded to a question about their stance on a potential new faith, saying they would be indifferent as long as it doesn't impose its beliefs on others.

Some users discussed dietary laws and their connections to faith. Anon noted that choosing to follow certain dietary laws is a matter of faith strength, while morality plays a role in deciding not to harm animals.

Another user shared their plan to recreate the Indus Valley Diet with modifications, which includes meat, seafood, and buffalo milk.

The thread also touched on the topic of genetics and 'muttification.' Haggu argued that genetic bottlenecks are not a significant concern, citing the example of Ashkenazi Jews.

The conversation was marked by disagreements and criticisms, with users expressing their opinions on faith, morality, and dietary laws.

articles.title: Anon Discusses Potential Indian Idol Group
articles.body:
Ambedkar started a thread asking if readers' sisters were auditioning for an Indian idol group called 'gobarjeeta kumari'. The group appears to be an offshoot of a Korean or Japanese group.

Dyaush and Anon expressed support for Northeastern Indians, with Dyaush mentioning a fond memory of a Nepali vendor and Anon stating that Northeastern Indians are superior.

Lungi described a hypothetical Indian idol as having sunken eyes, dark knees, and elbows, and predicted an 'aura loss' for Indian fans. Several respondents, including Mococo, Fuwawa, and Haggu, fantasized about dating and sexually exploiting idols.

Haggu and Mococo made graphic comments about rape and violence against women. Ozymandias and Motivanon discussed the potential pitfalls of creating an Indian idol group, including concerns about exploitation and the reaction of feminist organizations.

Gura suggested that the group could be organized through a vote system, similar to 4chan's approach to a Taylor Swift meetup. Bhondu urged patience, noting that the group had not yet been established.

Soyjeet later described a woman as looking 'trans' and compared North Indian and Pakistani men to the woman in question.

articles.title: Anon Calls for Violence Against Low Castes, Sparks Heated Replies
articles.body:
Soythie opened the thread with a post calling for violence against low castes, stating, 'Why aren’t hating low castes today?

Skin low castes alive!!' Anon, responding as Mococo, urged Soythie to 'calm down yaar pajeet,' and proposed a set of criteria to define a low caste, including being in the SC/ST category, refusing to identify as Hindu, and not being a Savarna.

Mococo emphasized that they were 'not Mudslimes.' Mococo further stated that bringing up issues with low castes was fine, but it should be done in a dedicated manner with effort.

They expressed concern about the impact of enabling certain figures, like Ambedkar, who they claimed 'shat on our nation.' Another poster, Bhondu, shared their views on low castes, stating that they 'suck India dry' by putting 'false cases on innocent ppl,' stealing 'seats in colleges and jobs from deserving ppl,' and spreading 'hate against us even after everything they get from us.' Bhondu suggested that caste kanging would only stop when low castes are rightfully removed from power and resources.

The discussion took a darker turn with Soythie responding with aggressive and threatening language towards pajeets and low castes.

Other posters, like Ozymandias and Chuddie, chimed in with their opinions, with Ozymandias calling out Soythie for defending pedophiles and Chuddie questioning the priorities of the discussion.

Mococo continued to engage with the topic, stating that it's fine as long as people can express their opinions, even if they are about low castes.

They also emphasized that their criticism is not about 'kanging' or mocking low castes, but about addressing their behavior.

The thread drew various reactions, with some posters expressing frustration and anger towards low castes, while others pushed back against what they saw as hate speech or pointless provocation.

articles.title: Anon Debates Sariya Meme and Rape Jokes
articles.body:
Haggu started the discussion, asking if others thought a sariya meme was justified and if they would stop fellow gang members from committing such acts.

Anon Nepajeet responded, saying they wouldn't find pleasure in someone's misery unless they had a reason to hate or despise that person.

Another anon, Gura, mentioned laughing at dark humor and absurd thoughts without condoning violence. Ambedkar stated that laughing about rape and murder of innocents is deplorable and subhuman.

Mococo brought up the origin of the sariya meme, referencing the Nirbhaya case, and questioned the hypocrisy of making jokes about sexual assault.

articles.title: Anon's Dreams Crushed, Replies Get Weird
articles.body:
Ambedkar opened the thread saying their dreams are being crushed every day and asked for motivational posts. Replies quickly took a turn for the bizarre. Ozymandias and Fuwawa started exchanging strange and somewhat nonsensical messages.

Fuwawa mentioned someone's belly being soft and then started ranting about not being able to get a certain name. The conversation further devolved with Ozymandias making a comment about Fuwawa's body and Fuwawa responding with anger.

Ambedkar also made some comments that seemed out of place.
